judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit expectations a, b, and c with comparable depth and citations; Report 2 additionally hits expectation d by explicitly recommending a global/env-level gate combined with the per-tenant flag, which Report 1 omits entirely.

report_1.evidence:
Reminders **will be sent automatically**. The deciding code is `auto_send_reminders_enabled` in `backend/app/feature_flags.py`, lines 11–12:

```python
if tenant.settings.get("auto_send_reminders") is None:
    return True
```

report_2.evidence: Reminders WILL be sent automatically for a clinic that never opened the setting.** For such a clinic, `tenant.settings` has no `auto_send_reminders` key, so `tenant.settings.get("auto_send_reminders")` returns `None`. That hits the branch at `backend/app/feature_flags.py:11-12`... The deciding function is `auto_send_reminders_enabled` and the deciding line is line 12 (`return True`).

report_1.evidence: No. Automatically sending SMS/email to patients is a consequential, outward-facing side effect... A safe default for a messaging feature is **off (opt-in)**... I would change it. In `backend/app/feature_flags.py:11–12`, flip the missing-value default to `False`

report_2.evidence: No — this default is wrong for this feature, and I would change it... For that category the default must invert: null/missing/unset should mean **off**... I would change `backend/app/feature_flags.py:11-13` to default off

report_1.evidence: SLOT-190 (`25509a7`) created `feature_flags.py` with `compact_calendar_view_enabled`, using the idiom "if the setting is `None`, return `True`." SLOT-214 (`aa4cf8d`) added `auto_send_reminders_enabled` as a **line-for-line copy of that same pattern**... Default-on is harmless for `compact_calendar_view`, a cosmetic display preference with no external effect. Reusing the same "unset means enabled" semantics for a feature that dispatches real messages to patients is a category error

report_2.evidence: It was copy-pasted from the calendar-view flag, and that was not a sound basis.** The git history shows `compact_calendar_view_enabled` was introduced in SLOT-190 (`25509a7`) with the `if ... is None: return True` grandfather-on shape. SLOT-214 (`aa4cf8d`) added `auto_send_reminders_enabled` as a byte-for-byte structural clone of that function... the two flags are in different blast-radius categories (harmless UI preference vs. automated outbound messaging)

Add an env-level master switch and AND it with the per-tenant value**, so the feature is a global no-op until deliberately turned on for the rollout — currently `reminders.py:25` gates on the tenant flag alone, with no master kill/enable switch anywhere in the repo (grep found none).
